methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Nucleus Genomics is a Series A biotech company founded by Kian Sadeghi that has raised $32M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 8 private and 3 public companies in Biotech & Health, including Series B comparables such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression modeling applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $64M. Peer-median anchoring across the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces an $805M implied value, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.6x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) is not applicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies. The triangulated analysis yields a low of $54M, median of $434M, and high of $926M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around growth trajectory and competitive positioning relative to later-stage peers commanding substantially higher valuations. The stage-progression floor and peer-median ceiling mark reasonable bounds; the median estimate represents the central tendency but should be weighted against execution risk and the heterogeneity of peer outcomes in this sector.
